MU.headline: Two-quarter beat-and-raise cadence accelerating — Q2 revenue beat guide by 28%, EPS by 45%; Q3 guide implies further +40% QoQ.

shifts.note: QoQ growth re-accelerated from +21% to +75% and is guided to +40% again
shifts.trend: Revenue $13.6B → $23.9B actual → $33.5B guide (YoY: +57% → +196% → implied +250%+)

shifts.note: Pricing power compounding — AI demand outpaces industry supply
shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM 56.8% → 74.9% → ~81% guide (≈ +24 pts of expansion in two quarters)

shifts.note: Beat magnitude widening, not narrowing — credibility on the raised Q3 guide is high
shifts.trend: Q2 revenue guided $18.7B → printed $23.9B (+28% beat); Q3 now guided to $33.5B (+40% QoQ)

shifts.note: Same constraint language for two consecutive quarters; tightness is persistent, not transient
shifts.trend: 'Supply allocation decisions may impact certain customers' (Q1) → 'Industry supply allocation decisions continue' (Q2) — no easing language added

shifts.note: HBM hyperscaler reliance increasing — single-customer dependency to monitor
shifts.trend: One customer reached 13% of six-month revenue (Q2 disclosure) — primarily CMBU (HBM/hyperscale)

shifts.note: Singapore HBM advanced-packaging fab broke ground; capacity additions from CY2027
shifts.trend: FY26 capex ~$20B (Q1 guide); Q1 capex $5.39B + Q2 capex $5.0B = $10.4B run-rate H1 — on pace for $20B+

shifts.note: First meaningful dividend hike in years; mgmt signaling cycle durability
shifts.trend: Dividend $0.115/share (Q1) → $0.15/share (Q2, +30%)

MU.verdict: Thesis intact and strengthening — beat-and-raise cadence is widening, gross-margin expansion is compounding, and guidance credibility is high. The only emerging risk is HBM customer concentration (one customer at 13% of revenue). Supply tightness language has not eased — bullish for memory pricing through CY2026.

NVDA.headline: DC growth holding ~+66–68% YoY; customer concentration formally disclosed at 4-way >10%; China is now effectively zero, but supply/energy commentary unchanged.

shifts.note: Slight re-acceleration on a full-year basis after Blackwell ramp
shifts.trend: DC YoY +66% (Q3 FY26) → +68% (FY26 full year); Networking +162% on GB200/GB300 NVLink ramp

shifts.note: Hyperscaler dependence newly visible — diligence risk-additive
shifts.trend: Q3 disclosed 4 direct customers each >10% of revenue (22/15/13/11%); 10-K did not previously break this out

shifts.note: China optionality fully impaired across both filings
shifts.trend: Q3 China $3.0B vs $8.1B prior-year Q3; H20 effectively zero by FY26 after $4.5B Q1 inventory charge; Feb 2026 H200 license carries 25% US import tariff
Non-GAAP GM 73.4% (Q3) → 73.5% guide (Q1 FY27); compressed YoY on Blackwell full-system mix but holding within target

shifts.note: Quote nearly identical across the two filings
shifts.trend: Both filings cite data-center / energy / capital availability as the gating constraint on customer deployments — no easing language added

shifts.note: Vendor-financing footprint growing — watch FCF translation
shifts.trend: FY26 10-K disclosed $17.5B in private/infra investments plus $3.5B land/power/shell guarantees to early-stage AI customers; Q3 added $7.5B new DC lease obligations FY26 Q4–FY30

NVDA.verdict: Story still about supply-constrained AI capex. The new disclosures (4-way customer concentration, vendor-financing footprint) are risk-additive but don't break the thesis; China optionality is now fully impaired and should be modeled at zero.

AMD.headline: Data Center re-accelerated to +57% YoY as MI350 GPU momentum builds; gross margin expanded and China business partially recovered with MI325 licenses despite 25% tariff.

shifts.trend: DC YoY: +32% (FY25) → +57% (Q1 FY26); total revenue +34% → +38%
shifts.note: MI350 GPU ramp driving the acceleration; MI308 China charges in FY25 created a difficult compare that is clearing

shifts.trend: Gross margin 50% (FY25) → 53% (Q1 FY26 +3pts YoY)
shifts.note: Richer Data Center mix driving the expansion; China export charge drag fading

shifts.trend: MI308 $440M net charge in FY25 → MI325 licenses granted Feb 2026 (with 25% tariff on US reimport)
shifts.note: A partial unlock — China business resumes but tariff limits margin contribution

shifts.trend: DC as % of revenue: ~48% (FY25 implied) → 56% (Q1 FY26); no customer >10% both periods
shifts.note: Data Center now majority of the business; OpenAI 6 GW deal locks in multi-year demand

shifts.trend: International revenue 66% (Q1 FY25) → 74% (Q1 FY26); no single customer >10% in either period
shifts.note: Geographic mix shifting toward international (hyperscaler GPU shipping locations) but customer concentration diversified

AMD.verdict: Thesis intact and improving — DC acceleration and margin expansion confirm MI350 cycle is real; key watch is whether MI325 China licenses scale or stall at the tariff barrier.

AMAT.headline: Top-line softened as trailing-edge foundry demand pulled back, but DRAM/HBM mix jumped from 26% to 34% of systems revenue — validating the HBM equipment thesis.

shifts.trend: Semi Systems: +4% (FY25) → -2% (Q1 FY26 YoY); total revenue $28.4B → $7.0B Q1 annualized ~$28B
shifts.note: Trailing-edge logic demand pulled back; China restrictions continue to weigh

shifts.trend: DRAM share of Semi Systems mix: 26% (FY25) → 34% (Q1 FY26); HBM and technology-transition spending driving the shift
shifts.note: The key bull case is playing out — DRAM/HBM becoming the dominant mix driver as foundry/logic softens

shifts.trend: China revenue: 30% of FY25 total → 24% nine-month FY26; absolute dollar decline QoQ on export controls
shifts.note: Taiwan (+71% FY25, +46% Q1) and Korea offset; overall geographic mix healthier

shifts.trend: Operating margin: ~26% FY25 → 21.8% Q1 FY26 (-4.3pts); $253M BIS settlement charge drove most of the decline
shifts.note: Mostly one-time; gross margin 49.0% (+0.2pts) was actually stable — operating hit was the BIS compliance settlement

shifts.trend: $253M BIS settlement in Q1 FY26 for prior China shipment violations; ongoing export-control complexity in China (24% of revenue Q1)
shifts.note: First major financial hit from BIS enforcement; manageable but signals compliance overhang

AMAT.verdict: Thesis mostly intact — HBM mix acceleration validates the equipment supercycle, but China restriction drag and the BIS settlement are real near-term headwinds to monitor.

AMKR.headline: Revenue re-accelerated sharply to +27.5% as all four end markets recovered; Arizona advanced packaging fab capex ramp to $2.5-3.0B confirms long-cycle US investment thesis.

shifts.trend: Revenue: +6.2% (FY25) → +27.5% (Q1 2026 YoY); all 4 end markets positive in Q1
shifts.note: FY25 base was soft due to Vietnam ramp costs and cycle; recovery broad-based

shifts.trend: Gross margin: 14.0% (FY25, -0.8pts YoY) → 14.2% Q1 2026 (+2.3pts YoY)
shifts.note: Factory utilization recovering on higher volumes; Vietnam ramp drag fading

shifts.trend: Capex: $904.6M (FY25, 13.5% of sales) → $2.5-3.0B guided FY26 (~37-45% of expected sales); Q1 26 already $224.6M vs $79.9M Q1 25
shifts.note: Driven by Arizona Facility (1.8M sq ft, manufacturing H1 2028); 3x capex increase signals long-cycle confidence

shifts.trend: Apple ~29.8% (FY25) and Qualcomm ~11.1% (FY25); top-10 = 72% both periods
shifts.note: Heavy Apple dependency unchanged — single largest risk if iPhone cycle softens

AMKR.verdict: Long-cycle capex ramp at Arizona validates the advanced packaging thesis, but returns depend on landing the next FCBGA/HBM-integration programs beyond the current Apple/Qualcomm base.

AMZN.headline: AWS re-accelerated to +28% and Q1 capex hit $43.2B (+78% YoY) as multi-hundred-billion RPO commitments locked in; operating margin expanded despite the buildout.

shifts.note: Re-acceleration in Q1 2026 after $128.7B full-year base — strong demand signal

shifts.trend: Capex: $128.3B (FY25) → $43.2B in Q1 2026 alone (+78% YoY for Q1)
shifts.note: Annualized Q1 run-rate ~$173B implies another step-up from already record FY25

shifts.trend: AWS-related RPO: $244B (Dec 31 2025) → $364B (Mar 31 2026) +49% in one quarter
shifts.note: OpenAI $100B expansion of existing $38B deal + $50B AMZN investment locked in; Anthropic another $25B

shifts.trend: AWS operating margin: 35.4% (FY25) → 37.7% (Q1 2026)
shifts.note: Margin expanding even as capex surges — business model efficiency is intact

AMZN.verdict: Strongest hyperscaler demand signal: AWS re-accelerating, margin expanding, and RPO surging — this is a bullish unlock for the entire inference infrastructure stack.

ANET.headline: Revenue growth accelerated to +35% as AI Ethernet trials convert to binding backlog, but gross margin compressed 2pts on large-customer mix and memory/silicon supply tightening.

shifts.note: Acceleration driven by AI Ethernet Scale-Out and Scale-Across deployments at Cloud Titans

shifts.trend: Gross margin: 64.1% (FY25) → 61.9% (Q1 FY26, -2.2pts)
shifts.note: Large-customer discount mix + memory/silicon supply tightening driving the compression; ANET procuring ahead to buffer

shifts.trend: Memory and silicon supply tightening cited Q1 FY26; deferred revenue $5.37B (FY25) → $6.20B (+$826M QoQ) as acceptance-period contracts accumulate
shifts.note: Deferred rev growth signals large trials in acceptance phase — revenue will recognize on deployment completion

shifts.trend: Americas mix: 79.7% (FY25) → 84.5% (Q1 FY26); top two end customers 26%/16% (FY25 disclosure) with binding backlog $968M at Q1
shifts.note: Growing dependence on US cloud titan order cadence; geographic concentration in Americas tightening

ANET.verdict: Thesis intact — AI Ethernet momentum is real, but the GM compression from large-customer pricing is a persistent headwind worth watching as trial volume scales to full deployment.

AVGO.headline: Custom AI accelerator revenue surged +52% in Q1 as top-five customer concentration jumped from 40% to 50% — XPU cycle is real but concentration risk has intensified.

shifts.trend: Semiconductor solutions: +22% (FY25) → +52% (Q1 FY26); total revenue +24% → +29%
shifts.note: XPU custom AI accelerators + AI networking (Tomahawk/Jericho) driving semiconductor acceleration

shifts.trend: Top-five end customers: ~40% of FY25 revenue → ~50% Q1 FY26; single distributor: 32% (FY25) → 42% (Q1 FY26)
shifts.note: One hyperscaler XPU customer effectively driving the quarterly print through a distributor channel; highest concentration ever disclosed

shifts.trend: Gross margin: 68% (FY25) → 68% (Q1 FY26) — flat despite semiconductor mix shift from 55% to 65% of revenue
shifts.note: Infrastructure software mix declining as semi surges, but semi GM held; overall GM resilient

shifts.trend: Semiconductor solutions as % of revenue: ~55% (FY25 Q1) → 65% (Q1 FY26); infrastructure software stable at $6.8B
shifts.note: Mix tilting toward semi, which historically carries lower margin than software; GM held this quarter but bears watching

AVGO.verdict: XPU cycle is delivering, but the 42% single-distributor concentration is the highest ever disclosed — thesis intact but binary risk from that one hyperscaler relationship has risen materially.

CAT.headline: Power & Energy re-accelerated to +22% YoY in Q1 with data-center prime power demand 'trending higher'; backlog grew from $51.2B at year-end to continued expansion, but tariff headwinds added $1.0B in Q1.

shifts.trend: P&E segment: +12% (FY25) → +22% (Q1 FY26); Power Generation sub-segment: +32% FY25 continuing into Q1
shifts.note: Data-center prime power demand driving the acceleration; both North America and China power generation cited

shifts.trend: Backlog $51.2B at YE25 (from $30.0B at YE24) continuing to grow; prime power orders 'trending higher' as DC customers seek alternative power solutions
shifts.note: Supply-constrained backlog dynamic typical of industrial cycle peaks; favorable for pricing

shifts.trend: FY26 revenue guide 'low double digit growth' maintained; tariff $2.2-2.4B for FY26 reiterated; Q2 tariff ~$700M
shifts.note: Top-line guide unchanged but tariff drag adds uncertainty to margin

shifts.trend: Operating margin: 18.1% (Q1 FY25) → 17.7% (Q1 FY26); $1.0B IEEPA tariff in Q1; $700M guided Q2
shifts.note: Tariffs are the primary margin headwind; refund recovery not deemed probable per SCOTUS IEEPA ruling

CAT.verdict: Prime power demand from data centers is a structural tailwind confirmed across both reports — but the tariff headwind on margins is real and persistent, making this a story of top-line resilience with earnings uncertainty.

CDNS.headline: Growth accelerated to +19% YoY in Q1 as China revenue surged +36% (vs +19% FY25) after BIS export controls were rescinded; backlog held above $8B.

shifts.note: Broad-based; Other Americas +87%, Japan +36%, EMEA +23% led by geography
BIS 'is-informed' letter imposed May 23 and rescinded July 2, 2025 — the whiplash is resolved; BIS 50%-affiliate rule (Sep 2025) flagged as next risk

shifts.trend: Backlog: $7.8B (FY25 year-end) → $8.0B (Q1 FY26); 53% → 55% near-term recognition
shifts.note: Backlog growing and near-term fraction rising — healthy demand signal

shifts.trend: Operating margin: 28% (FY25, pressured by $128.5M BIS/DOJ settlement) → 29% (Q1 FY26)
shifts.note: Settlement charge was FY25-specific; margins recovering to structural level in FY26

CDNS.verdict: China BIS risk has materially improved and margins are recovering from the one-time settlement charge — thesis is unlocking as the export-control overhang clears.

CEG.headline: Transformed into the world's largest private power producer via $22B Calpine acquisition (closed Jan 7, 2026) while locking in multi-decade nuclear contracts with hyperscalers.

shifts.trend: Generation capacity: ~23 GW nuclear + hydro (pre-Calpine) → 55 GW combined (post-Calpine, adds 21 GW gas + 730 MW geothermal + 800 MW battery)
shifts.note: Calpine adds dispatchable gas to complement nuclear baseload; transforms CEG into a full power platform

shifts.trend: Adjusted operating EPS: $8.67 (FY24) → $9.39 (FY25); GAAP NI declined on lower nuclear PTC YoY
shifts.note: Underlying earnings power improving; GAAP decline is a PTC accounting timing issue not a business deterioration

shifts.trend: Long-term contracts expanding: 20-yr Meta PPA for Clinton (Q3 25) + Crane (TMI) 20-yr Microsoft PPA + DOE $1B loan guarantee Nov 2025
shifts.note: Hyperscaler demand for 24/7 clean baseload locking up CEG's best nuclear assets for decades

shifts.trend: ~$22B Calpine acquisition consideration (50M shares + $4.5B cash) closed Jan 7, 2026; significant debt added
shifts.note: Leverage increased materially with Calpine; free cash flow from the combined entity will need to delever

CEG.verdict: The Calpine close is transformative and the AI/data-center nuclear demand signal is confirmed across multiple hyperscaler PPAs — thesis strengthens, but leverage from the acquisition is the key new risk to track.

CLS.headline: CCS segment accelerated to +76% YoY in Q1 on hyperscaler networking switch programs; the paused Enterprise AI compute program rebooted with +101% growth.

shifts.trend: CCS segment: +42% (FY25) → +76% (Q1 FY26); HPS networking: +81% (FY25) → +63% (Q1 FY26, still elevated); total revenue: ~$3.1B annual rate → $4.05B Q1 (+53%)
shifts.note: Enterprise AI compute program (paused 2025) rebooted at +101% as next-gen GPU AI/ML ramp

shifts.trend: HPS (networking switches) as % of total revenue: 29% (FY24) → 41% (FY25) → 42% (Q1 FY26); CCS now 80% of revenue
shifts.note: HPS mix stabilizing at ~42% as Enterprise also ramps; portfolio diversification improving

shifts.trend: CCS segment margin: 8.0% (Q1 FY25) → 8.6% (Q1 FY26)
shifts.note: Operating leverage on higher CCS volume; capex investment is margin-neutral short term

shifts.trend: Capex: historical 1.5-2.0% of revenue → ~$1B (~6% of revenue) guided FY26; revolver upsized from $750M to $1.75B in Q1
shifts.note: 3x+ capex intensity increase signals long-cycle capacity confidence from hyperscaler demand visibility

shifts.trend: 3 CCS customers each >10% of revenue (32%/14%/12%) FY25; structure unchanged in Q1
shifts.note: High concentration persists; Enterprise customer resumption at +101% adds some diversity but still top-heavy

CLS.verdict: Thesis intact and re-accelerating — both HPS networking and Enterprise compute are growing simultaneously for the first time, though the 32%/14%/12% customer concentration remains the single-point-of-failure risk.

CMI.headline: Power Systems EBITDA margin expanded from 22.7% to 29.5% in one quarter on data-center-driven Power Generation demand; DC backlog cited as extending 6-8 quarters.

shifts.trend: Power Systems: +16% (FY25, $7.46B) → +19% (Q1 FY26, $1.96B YoY); Power Generation: +19% (FY25) → +28% (Q1 FY26)
shifts.note: Data-center prime power demand driving consecutive acceleration; North America and China both cited as growth regions

shifts.trend: Power Systems EBITDA margin: 18.4% (FY24) → 22.7% (FY25) → 29.5% (Q1 FY26)
shifts.note: Three consecutive periods of margin expansion on operating leverage; pricing favorable on supply-constrained market

shifts.trend: Data center product demand extending 6-8 quarters out (FY25 10-K) → continuing strong in Q1 FY26; supply-constrained per language in both reports
shifts.note: 6-8 quarter visibility is exceptionally long for an industrial company; signals demand far outpacing capacity

CMI.verdict: High-conviction supply-constrained industrial play on data center power — the 6-8 quarter demand visibility and 700bps EBITDA margin expansion in Q1 are the strongest signals in the industrial stack.

COHR.headline: Datacenter & Communications accelerated to +41% in FQ3 as a new 'scale across' interconnect category emerged alongside AI datacenter and DCI demand; gross margin expanded 243bps.

shifts.trend: D&C segment: +34% (FQ2 FY26) → +41% (FQ3 FY26); total revenue +17% → +21%
shifts.note: AI datacenter + DCI + telecom all cited as concurrent growth drivers in FQ2; 'scale across' explicitly named as fourth driver in FQ3

shifts.trend: Gross margin: 37% (+140bps YoY, FQ2) → 38% (+243bps YoY, FQ3)
shifts.note: Sustained margin expansion from yield improvements and pricing optimization; D&C mix shift helps

shifts.trend: D&C segment profit: +44% (FQ2) → +49% (FQ3); 'scale across' inter-DC interconnect first named as a growth category in FQ3
shifts.note: Addition of 'scale across' signals hyperscaler AI fabric demand is expanding beyond intra-DC to inter-DC scale

shifts.trend: Portfolio narrowing: aerospace divested Sep 2025, Munich divested Jan 2026; industrial declining; D&C growing to larger share
shifts.note: Positive concentration in the right segment — divesting non-AI businesses and focusing on AI optics

COHR.verdict: Accelerating demand across intra-DC, DCI, and now 'scale across' confirms COHR is riding the full optics cycle upgrade — thesis intact and strengthening.

CRM.headline: Full-year RPO recovered to $72.4B (+14%) after a Q3 dip to $59.5B; cRPO +16% signals near-term demand acceleration and Informatica adds agentic data management.

shifts.trend: Revenue: +9% (Q3 FY26 quarterly) → +10% (FY26 full year, $41.5B)
shifts.note: Growth consistent but not yet re-accelerating; Agentforce consumption model revenue still early-stage

shifts.trend: RPO: $59.5B (mid-year low, Q3 FY26) → $72.4B (FY26 year-end, +14% YoY); cRPO $35.1B (+16%)
shifts.note: RPO recovered strongly from the mid-year dip; cRPO growing faster than total RPO signals near-term acceleration

shifts.trend: Operating cash flow: ~$13.1B (FY25) → $15.0B (FY26, +15%); $12.7B stock repurchases + $1.6B dividends in FY26
shifts.note: Cash return engine is firing; operating cash flow expanding faster than revenue

shifts.trend: Agentforce branding unified in Q3 FY26; Informatica $9.6B closed Nov 2025 for agentic data management; Agentforce 360 Platform as the unified AI/CRM stack
shifts.note: Platform strategy consolidating: Data Cloud (Snowflake competitor) + Informatica (ETL/governance) + Agentforce (AI agents)

CRM.verdict: RPO recovery and cRPO acceleration are the bull signal — but the 10% revenue growth rate means the Agentforce AI cycle hasn't yet meaningfully moved the needle; watch cRPO in Q1 FY27.

CRWV.headline: RPO surged from $60.7B to $98.8B in a single quarter as Microsoft concentration fell to 45% and Meta emerged at 20%; revenue growth slowed to +112% on a larger base.

shifts.note: Slowdown from base effects, not demand loss; RPO and customer diversification both improving

shifts.trend: Microsoft % of revenue: 77% (top-2 FY24) → 67% (FY25) → 45% (Q1 2026); Meta emerges at 20% Q1 2026
shifts.note: Microsoft dependency declining as OpenAI, Meta, and others scale — customer diversification is the key risk mitigation

shifts.trend: RPO: $60.7B (Dec 31 2025) → $98.8B (Mar 31 2026), +63% in one quarter; 36% recognized in first 24 months, 39% in months 25-48
shifts.note: Near-doubling of RPO in one quarter confirms hyperscaler willingness to commit multi-year capacity at scale

shifts.trend: Total debt: $21.6B (FY25) → +$7.8B more added post-Q1 (convertible notes + senior notes + equity); debt structure remains high-yield
shifts.note: Capital structure is the central risk — $21.6B pre-Q1 at avg ~10% rates; growth requires persistent refinancing

CRWV.verdict: Customer diversification is improving and RPO growth is extraordinary — but the leverage stack ($21.6B+ at ~10% avg) means the bull case requires sustained demand and low refinancing risk; any hyperscaler pullback is highly consequential.

CSCO.headline: Networking re-accelerated to +21% in Q2 as AI Infrastructure + Data Center Switching drove hyperscaler demand; Silicon One purchase commitments continue building.

shifts.trend: Total revenue: +8% (Q1 FY26) → +10% (Q2 FY26); Networking product category: +15% (Q1) → +21% (Q2)
shifts.note: Consecutive acceleration driven by AI Infrastructure, Data Center Switching, Service Provider Routing, and Campus

shifts.trend: Networking: +15% Q1 → +21% Q2 (+$1.4B); Security: flat Q1 → -4% Q2 on Splunk on-prem → cloud subscription migration
shifts.note: Diverging: Networking is the AI-driven growth engine while Security faces revenue model transition headwinds

shifts.trend: Silicon One purchase commitments and inventory both building Q1 and Q2; explicit hyperscaler demand driving the commitment buildup
shifts.note: Purchase commitment buildup raises inventory obsolescence risk but also signals strong demand visibility for hyperscaler AI fabric

shifts.trend: Consistent buybacks ($2.0B Q1) and dividends ($1.6B Q1); diluted EPS +31% Q2 on lower tax rate
shifts.note: Cash return engine stable; product GM improving on productivity despite mix

CSCO.verdict: Networking acceleration is confirming the AI switching cycle — but Security's Splunk migration drag creates a near-term earnings headwind; the net picture is bullish for the AI infrastructure thesis.

ETN.headline: Electrical Americas backlog surged to +44% YoY with organic orders +42% in Q1; Boyd Thermal acquisition ($9.5B, liquid cooling) pending close adds a new product layer.

shifts.trend: Electrical Americas: +16% (FY25) → +20% (Q1 FY26); Electrical Global: +9% (FY25) → +21% (Q1 FY26)
shifts.note: Both electrical segments accelerated simultaneously — data center demand spread globally

shifts.trend: Electrical Americas backlog: $13.2B (+31% YoY organic, FY25 year-end) → $14.5B (+44% YoY organic, Q1 FY26); customer orders +42% organic in Q1
shifts.note: Backlog growth is accelerating, not decelerating — demand running well ahead of supply capacity

shifts.trend: Electrical Americas operating margin: 29.9% (FY25) → 25.6% (Q1 FY26, -430bps)
shifts.note: Commodity/wage inflation driving the compression; pricing/volume only partially offset; not structural — backlog build supports future pricing

shifts.trend: Boyd Thermal acquisition $9.5B signed FY25 → pending close; adds liquid cooling (chip to grid coverage); recent additions: Fibrebond (modular enclosures), Resilient (solid-state transformers)
shifts.note: Portfolio expanding into liquid cooling via Boyd; Eaton positioning as full data center power + cooling infrastructure vendor

ETN.verdict: Backlog acceleration to +44% YoY with organic orders +42% is the strongest demand signal in the power infrastructure space — Boyd Thermal close would add the liquid cooling pillar that completes the DC infrastructure thesis.

FN.headline: DCI revenue surged +90.4% YoY in FQ3 as 1.6T-cycle inter-datacenter AI traffic accelerates; HPC line grew sequentially from $85.6M to $106.7M confirming AI compute assembly expansion.

shifts.trend: Total revenue: +35.9% (FQ2 FY26) → +39.3% (FQ3 FY26) YoY; 9M revenue $3.33B +32.5%
shifts.note: Both quarters strong; the mix within growth is shifting toward higher-value AI-relevant lines
DCI is the fastest-growing optical line as inter-DC AI scale-across fabric ramps for 1.6T interconnects; HPC steadily building

shifts.trend: Gross margin: 12.2% (FQ2) → 11.9% (FQ3, -30bps); operating margin 10.1% → 9.9%
shifts.note: Contract manufacturer margins structurally thin; slight compression on inventory build for Chonburi expansion

shifts.trend: 4 customers each >10% of revenue in both FQ2 and FQ3 (combined ~59%)
shifts.note: Nokia/Infinera + Lumentum + Cisco/Acacia consolidation flagged as risk — if those customers merge further, top-10 contracts

FN.verdict: DCI's +90% growth and HPC's sequential gains confirm Fabrinet is a direct beneficiary of the AI scale-across and HPC compute build — thesis intact and accelerating on the right product lines.

GEV.headline: Total RPO surged 32% YoY to $163.3B; Power EBITDA margin expanded from 14.7% to 16.3% as HA-Turbine orders paced above prior year; Prolec GE acquisition closed adding transformer capacity.

shifts.trend: Total RPO: $150.2B (+26% YoY, FY25 year-end) → $163.3B (+32% YoY, Q1 FY26); equipment RPO $64.2B → $75.9B in one quarter
shifts.note: Equipment RPO growing faster than services RPO — means more near-term delivery commitments, not just long-tail services

shifts.trend: Gas Power RPO: +29% YoY (FY25) → +30% YoY (Q1 FY26); HA-Turbine orders 25 (FY24) → 43 (FY25) → Q1 FY26 pacing 12 units vs 8 prior year
shifts.note: Order cadence re-accelerating from an already elevated base

shifts.trend: Power segment EBITDA margin: 12.5% (FY24) → 14.7% (FY25) → 16.3% (Q1 FY26)
shifts.note: Three consecutive periods of margin expansion on favorable pricing + higher HA-Turbine deliveries; pricing power confirmed

shifts.trend: Prolec GE (transformer JV) full buyout closed Feb 2026; Electrification RPO also growing on HVDC/substation demand alongside transformer
shifts.note: Transformers are a key bottleneck for data center grid connections — Prolec GE adds strategic capacity

GEV.verdict: RPO acceleration, margin expansion, and HA-Turbine order cadence all confirm GEV is the prime beneficiary of the data-center power infrastructure cycle — thesis strongly intact.

GNRC.headline: Pivoted from -2% full-year revenue to +12% in Q1 as C&I segment grew +28% on data center demand; large-megawatt backup generator market characterized as 'significantly supply-constrained'.

shifts.trend: Total revenue: -2% (FY25, $4.21B) → +12% (Q1 FY26, $1.06B YoY); C&I segment: +4.9% (FY25) → +28% (Q1 FY26)
shifts.note: Recovery from quiet-hurricane-season residential softness in FY25; C&I/data center demand the engine

shifts.trend: Adjusted EBITDA margin: 17.0% (FY25) → residential 25.1% / C&I 13.0% (Q1 FY26 under new structure); EBITDA $193M Q1 +29%
shifts.note: Residential segment margin improved 480bps on pricing/efficiencies; C&I improving but still product-mix-constrained

shifts.trend: Large-megawatt backup generator market 'significantly supply-constrained' per FY25 10-K; Enercon acquired Q1 FY26 for custom power equipment for hyperscale/enterprise DCs
shifts.note: Supply constraint is a positive signal for pricing power and multi-year order visibility

shifts.trend: Segment reorganization in Q1 FY26: Domestic/International → Residential/C&I; Enercon acquisition adds hyperscale custom power to C&I
shifts.note: Strategic realignment toward C&I/data center; new segment structure makes DC revenue more transparent going forward

GNRC.verdict: The data center C&I pivot is real and accelerating — supply-constrained market and new large-megawatt products position GNRC well, but residential weather dependency remains the swing factor.

GOOGL.headline: Google Cloud re-accelerated to +63% YoY in Q1 with operating margin expanding to 33%; RPO nearly doubled to $467.6B on multi-GW TPU supply agreements — the biggest single-quarter RPO jump of any hyperscaler.

shifts.trend: Google Cloud revenue: +36% (FY25, $58.7B) → +63% (Q1 2026, $20.0B YoY)
shifts.note: Significant re-acceleration; Cloud is now the fastest-growing major hyperscaler cloud in absolute terms

shifts.trend: Google Cloud operating margin: ~23.7% implied (FY25, $13.9B op income / $58.7B) → 33% (Q1 2026, $6.6B / $20.0B)
shifts.note: Cloud margin at 33% and 3x YoY is extraordinary — unit economics improving dramatically as scale absorbs infrastructure cost

shifts.trend: Capex: $52.5B (FY24) → $91.4B (FY25, +74%) → $35.7B (Q1 2026 alone, ~$143B annualized run rate)
shifts.note: 'Significantly increase' guidance from FY25 10-K already being executed; Q1 alone +74% YoY

shifts.trend: Revenue backlog (RPO): $242.8B (Dec 31 2025) → $467.6B (Mar 31 2026, +93% in one quarter); multi-GW TPU hardware supply agreements first disclosed
shifts.note: Multi-GW TPU deals are a new model: Google selling/leasing its own AI chips to third-party DCs; revenue starts late 2026

GOOGL.verdict: Cloud re-acceleration to +63% with 33% op margin and $467.6B RPO makes GOOGL the strongest hyperscaler demand signal right now — this is a material unlock for the AI infrastructure thesis.

HPE.headline: Juniper integration boosted Networking to +151.5% in Q1 as gross margin expanded 670bps; Cloud & AI servers growing but carry 'limited margins', creating a structural mix tension.

shifts.trend: Total revenue: +13.8% (FY25, 4 months Juniper) → +18.4% (Q1 FY26, full Juniper quarter); Networking: +51.1% (FY25) → +151.5% (Q1 FY26)
shifts.note: Q1 YoY comparison will normalize as Juniper laps its full-quarter contributions; underlying demand growth more like 15-20%

shifts.trend: Gross margin: ~29% (FY25 structure) → 35.9% (Q1 FY26, +670bps) on Juniper software/service mix
shifts.note: Juniper's software revenue structurally improves HPE's gross margin profile; one of the key rationales for the $13.4B acquisition

shifts.trend: Networking +151.5% (full Juniper) vs Cloud & AI -2.7% (AI server volume/AUP decline); AI servers growing in units but GPUs carry 'limited margins'
shifts.note: AI server growth at thin margin is a known tension; Networking is the margin engine, not AI servers

shifts.trend: $600M Juniper synergy target by FY28 with $800M investment; $350M Catalyst cost program targeting FY27 savings; $275M charges in FY25
shifts.note: Integration investment is front-loaded; synergy capture begins materializing from FY26 onward

HPE.verdict: Juniper acquisition thesis is playing out in margins — but AI server growth at 'limited margins' means HPE's AI Infrastructure story is more nuanced than a pure demand play.

HUBB.headline: Revenue growth tripled from +4% to +11% as Electrical Solutions accelerated to +11.8% on datacenter and T&D market strength; adjusted operating margin expanded 110bps.

shifts.trend: Total revenue: +3.8% (FY25, $5.84B) → +11% (Q1 FY26, $1.52B YoY); Electrical Solutions: +7.1% (FY25) → +11.8% (Q1 FY26, organic +10.6%)
shifts.note: Data center and T&D market together driving the organic acceleration; light industrial also contributing

shifts.trend: Adjusted operating margin: ~22.5% (FY25) → expanded +110bps (Q1 FY26)
shifts.note: Pricing/productivity outpacing cost inflation even with tariff headwinds; pricing discipline confirmed

shifts.trend: Backlog: $2.16B (FY25 year-end, +14% YoY) → continues growing into Q1 FY26; substantially all to ship in 2026
shifts.note: Backlog growth rate (+14% YoY) exceeding revenue growth — demand pipeline building ahead of production

HUBB.verdict: Datacenter demand is pulling Electrical Solutions into a higher growth regime — thesis intact as pricing power holds even with cost/tariff headwinds.

INTC.headline: DCAI re-accelerated to +22% on supply-constrained premium pricing but Intel Foundry continued losing $2.4B in Q1 alone; supply constraints persist through H1 2026.

shifts.note: Demand-based pricing on supply-constrained Intel 7 and 18A wafers driving DCAI ASP improvement +27% YoY

shifts.trend: Intel 7 supply constrained Q4 2025 (10-K) → supply constraints expected to persist 'at least through H1 2026'; substrate and memory shortages added in Q1 10-Q
shifts.note: Supply tightness has become more broad-based — now includes substrate and memory components, not just Intel 7 wafer fab

shifts.trend: Intel Foundry operating loss: -$10.3B (FY25) → -$2.4B (Q1 2026 alone, on pace for $9-10B annually); external revenue $307M (FY25) → $174M (Q1 2026)
shifts.note: Foundry is the structural drag; 18A ramp costs are elevated and external revenue is tiny; the may-pause-14A language was the key strategic admission

shifts.trend: FY25 restructuring ~$1.6B+ → Q1 2026 restructuring $4.1B in a single quarter; SoftBank $2B + NVIDIA $5B equity investments provide capital but dilute
shifts.note: $4.1B Q1 restructuring on top of FY25 charges signals ongoing workforce reduction scale

INTC.verdict: The Foundry losses continue at scale and the 14A strategic risk is real — DCAI premium-pricing recovery is positive but doesn't offset Foundry; thesis is structurally challenged until an external Foundry customer materializes.

KLAC.headline: Q3 was a record quarter at $3.42B (+11% YoY) as Korea memory investment surged +80%; China declined to 24% of revenue as HBM/DRAM drives the geographic mix.

shifts.trend: Revenue: +7% (Q2 FY26, $3.30B) → +11% (Q3 FY26, $3.42B, record quarter)
shifts.note: Revenue acceleration driven by memory/DRAM; foundry/logic steady

shifts.trend: Korea revenue: +34% YoY (Q2) → +80% YoY (Q3, $681M); memory DRAM / HBM investment clearly accelerating
shifts.note: Korea +80% is the signal — HBM-related process control intensity driving extraordinary investment

shifts.trend: China revenue: 30.2% of Q2 revenue (-9% YoY) → 24% of Q3 revenue (nine-month flat at $3.09B)
shifts.note: China mix shrinking as export controls constrain; Taiwan and Korea offset; geographic mix is healthier for compliance risk

shifts.trend: Gross margin: 61.4% (Q2) → 61.1% (Q3, -30bps); DRAM chip cost for image computers cited as transitory headwind
shifts.note: Margin essentially flat; DRAM cost headwind is temporary (same DRAM supercycle driving revenue also raising internal tooling costs)

KLAC.verdict: HBM-driven Korea memory investment is the bull case fully materializing — record quarter with geographic mix shifting away from China is precisely the investment thesis playing out.

LEU.headline: Backlog reached $3.9B and Fluor EPC contract signed for Piketon LEU+HALEU expansion; Oklo JV exploration adds nuclear fuel cycle optionality, but advanced technology costs jumped +530%.

shifts.trend: Backlog: $3.8B (FY25 year-end) → $3.9B (Q1 FY26); contingent LEU contracts $2.3B → $2.4B (all pending capacity)
shifts.note: Backlog growing despite slow near-term deliveries; $2.4B contingent contracts represent future demand pending new capacity build

shifts.trend: Advanced technology costs Q1: $3.0M (Q1 FY25) → $18.9M (Q1 FY26, +530%); Fluor EPC contract signed Feb 2026 for Piketon commercial expansion
shifts.note: Spending phase ramping dramatically; Oak Ridge centrifuge manufacturing + Piketon EPC contract commits ~$560M+ investment

shifts.trend: Q1 net income: $27.2M (Q1 FY25) → $10.0M (Q1 FY26); advanced tech costs drove the decline
shifts.note: Investment-phase earnings compression is expected; LEU SWU spot price $200 (from $34 historic low) supports long-run margin recovery

shifts.trend: $900M DOE HALEU task order signed Jan 5, 2026; Oklo JV exploration announced March 2026; NRC license for up to 20% U-235 HALEU enrichment maintained
shifts.note: Federal government support is strengthening through the DOE task order; Oklo partnership creates demand pull for HALEU

LEU.verdict: Pre-revenue infrastructure investment thesis — the DOE $900M task order and $2.4B contingent contract backlog confirm the demand is real; the investment-phase earnings compression is the expected cost of building capacity.

LITE.headline: Revenue surged +90% YoY in FQ3 as laser chips at 200G lane speed re-rated gross margin to 44.2% (+1,540bps YoY); supply allocation language hardened from 'led to' to 'required'.

shifts.note: Consecutive quarter acceleration on 200G-lane InP laser chip ramp for 1.6T transceivers

shifts.trend: Gross margin: 36.1% +1,130bps YoY (FQ2) → 44.2% +1,540bps YoY (FQ3); +810bps QoQ sequential in one quarter
shifts.note: Supply allocation + 200G lane-speed mix shift structurally re-rating margins; not a one-time effect — continuing to expand

shifts.trend: Supply language: 'demand is outpacing supply which has led to decisions on supply allocation' (FQ2) → 'has required us to make decisions on supply allocation' (FQ3)
shifts.note: Language hardening confirms allocation decisions are now systematic, not occasional; LITE is the InP/EML supply choke point

shifts.trend: Optical circuit switches: >$10M initial shipments (FQ2) → >$25M (FQ3, +150% QoQ); OCS is a brand-new product line in AI switch fabrics
shifts.note: OCS is early but growing fast — direct exposure to AI switch-fabric architecture evolution

LITE.verdict: LITE is at a structural inflection — supply-constrained laser chips at 200G are re-rating the business, and OCS adds optionality; this is the clearest supply-bottleneck play in the optics layer.

LRCX.headline: Revenue accelerated +9% QoQ as DRAM/HBM investment pushed memory mix from 34% to 39%; Korea and Taiwan combined at 46% of revenue as China held at 34%.

shifts.trend: Revenue: $5.34B flat QoQ (Q2 FY26) → $5.84B +9% QoQ (Q3 FY26); nine-month $16.51B vs $13.26B +24% YoY
shifts.note: Sequential acceleration confirms the DRAM/HBM equipment investment cycle is building, not plateauing

shifts.trend: Memory mix: 34% of systems revenue (Q2) → 39% (Q3); DRAM specifically driving; Foundry 59% → 54% as mature node slows
shifts.note: Memory/DRAM re-weighting is the bull thesis — HBM-driven technology transitions are the most WFE-intensive in history

shifts.trend: Gross margin: 49.6% (Q2 FY26) → 49.8% (Q3 FY26, +20bps)
shifts.note: Margins holding on better factory efficiencies; customer mix slightly favorable in Q3

shifts.trend: China revenue: 35% (Q2) → 34% (Q3); absolute level steady
shifts.note: China holding near the export-control floor; not declining further but also not recovering meaningfully

LRCX.verdict: DRAM/HBM cycle confirmation is the key signal — sequential revenue acceleration + rising memory mix validate the WFE investment cycle thesis with no signs of demand deceleration.

META.headline: Revenue re-accelerated to +33% YoY as Q1 capex guidance was raised to $125-145B (from $115-135B) and non-cancelable commitments reached $237.7B — the largest single-quarter AI infrastructure commitment signal.

shifts.trend: Revenue: +22% (FY25, $201.0B) → +33% (Q1 2026, $56.3B YoY); ad impressions +12% (FY25) → +19% (Q1); price per ad +9% → +12%
shifts.note: Both volume and pricing improving simultaneously; AI-driven ad relevance cited as a driver

shifts.trend: Capex: $72.2B (FY25) → $125-145B guided FY26; RAISED from $115-135B at year-end; Q1 2026 alone $19.8B
shifts.note: Mid-year guide raise signals demand confidence; $125-145B FY26 implies Q2-Q4 pacing even higher than Q1

shifts.trend: Non-cancelable contractual commitments: $237.7B at Mar 31 2026 (mostly cloud capacity, servers, DCs); $182.9B off-balance-sheet leases not yet commenced
shifts.note: Total committed obligation approaching $420B — signals decade-long infrastructure buildout visibility

shifts.trend: FoA operating income tracking revenue growth; Reality Labs loss $4.03B Q1 (FY25 pace $19.2B annually, flat guidance)
shifts.note: Core business margins holding while RL absorbs AI/metaverse losses; overall mix stable

META.verdict: META's capex raise mid-year and $237.7B committed infrastructure obligations are the single strongest hyperscaler AI demand signal — this is a direct bullish unlock for GPU, networking, power, and cooling layers.

MOD.headline: Climate Solutions data center revenue grew from +$67.4M to +$130M YoY as CS segment hit +51% growth; Gentherm RMT announced to create a pure-play data center cooling company.

shifts.trend: Climate Solutions: +24% (Q2 FY26) → +51% (Q3 FY26); DC product contribution: +$67.4M YoY (Q2) → +$130.0M nine-month cumulative (Q3)
shifts.note: DC revenue doubling YoY on a growing nine-month cumulative base — hyperscale and colocation in NA and Europe both cited

shifts.trend: CS operating margin: 17.6% (prior-year Q2 base) → 13.7% (Q2 FY26) → 15.3% (Q3 FY26); well below prior 17%+ peak
shifts.note: Margin compressed by rapid capacity expansion inefficiencies — recovering but not back to peak; management expects improvement as capacity matures

shifts.trend: Reverse Morris Trust announced Jan 2026: Performance Technologies to merge with Gentherm (~$1B value); MOD retains Climate Solutions as pure-play DC + HVAC/refrigeration
shifts.note: Strategic simplification to pure-play DC cooling is the right move — removes PT segment margin drag and signals conviction in the DC thesis

shifts.trend: $100M incremental DC capex announced (Q2 FY26); DC manufacturing lease signed (Q3 FY26); multiple acquisitions (AbsolutAire, LB White, Climate by Design) in FY26
shifts.note: Accelerating investment in CS capacity confirms management confidence in sustained DC demand

MOD.verdict: The Gentherm RMT and pure-play DC pivot validates the cooling thesis — but the CS margin compression from rapid capacity ramp is the near-term risk that must resolve before the full re-rating happens.

MRVL.headline: Data Center accelerated to +46% in FY26 (from +38% in Q3) and gross margin expanded 9.7pts as Celestial AI (photonic fabric) and XConn (PCIe/CXL) acquisitions closed, completing the custom AI ASIC platform.

shifts.trend: Data Center: +38% YoY (Q3 FY26) → +46% YoY (FY26 full year, $6.1B); total revenue +37% Q3 → +42% FY26
shifts.note: Full-year acceleration confirms Q3 was not the peak; MRVL custom AI ASIC cycle is building

shifts.trend: Gross margin: ~23% (prior-year Q3 after $356.8M restructuring impairments) → 51.6% (Q3 FY26) → 51.0% (FY26 full year)
shifts.note: Prior year was severely depressed by $358M impairments; organic margin also improving on higher DC mix and cost absorption

shifts.trend: Distributor A: 34% of FY25 revenue → 37% of FY26 revenue; Direct Customer A 13% → 14%
shifts.note: Concentration tightening as the large hyperscaler XPU program scales; similar risk profile to AVGO

shifts.trend: DC as % of total: 73% (Q3 FY26) → 74% (FY26); Communications consolidated to single line; Celestial AI + XConn closed adding photonics and CXL/PCIe switching
shifts.note: Portfolio now unified around custom AI ASICs + SerDes + silicon photonics + HBM integration + CXL switching — the full AI silicon stack

MRVL.verdict: Celestial + XConn acquisitions complete the custom AI silicon platform — thesis is intact and accelerating, but 37% distributor concentration is the same concentration risk as AVGO, just one rung below.

MSFT.headline: Azure re-accelerated to +40% in Q3 (from +39% in Q2) while Q3 capex hit $30.9B; commercial RPO held at $627B as recognition pace kept up with new signings.

shifts.trend: Azure: +39% (Q2 FY26) → +40% (Q3 FY26); Microsoft Cloud revenue: +26% (Q2) → +29% (Q3)
shifts.note: Azure re-acceleration — not decelerating even from 39% base; AI workload demand still expanding

shifts.trend: Quarterly capex: $29.9B (Q2 FY26) → $30.9B (Q3 FY26); 9M total $80.1B vs $40.9B prior year nine months (+96%)
shifts.note: Almost doubling capex YoY with no sign of pullback; $80.1B in 9 months exceeds total FY25 run rate

shifts.trend: Commercial RPO: $631B (Q2 end) → $627B (Q3 end); new signings approximately matching recognition rate
shifts.note: RPO essentially flat suggests no slowdown in new bookings — recognition pace is keeping up with demand

shifts.trend: Microsoft Cloud gross margin: stable at ~67% both quarters; AI infrastructure investments cited as the compression driver
shifts.note: 67% Cloud GM is still excellent but below prior peaks — infra investment is the structural drag

MSFT.verdict: Azure re-acceleration to 40% while maintaining $627B RPO and nearly doubling capex YoY is the most straightforward bull confirmation — thesis strongly intact.

MXL.headline: Revenue accelerated to +43% YoY in Q1 on optical and wireless backhaul/access infrastructure strength; customer concentration eased significantly as top-10 share fell from 65% to 56%.

shifts.trend: Revenue: +30% (FY25, $467.6M) → +43% (Q1 2026, $137.2M YoY); Infrastructure line: +$34.3M (FY25 full year) → +$36.3M (Q1 2026 alone)
shifts.note: Q1 alone generated more Infrastructure growth than all of FY25 — optical DSP cycle is building

shifts.trend: Top-10 customers: 65% (FY25) → 56% (Q1 2026); top customer: >20% (FY25 range) → 13% (Q1 2026)
shifts.note: Customer base broadening is positive for durability; reduces single-customer risk meaningfully

shifts.trend: Gross margin: 57% +3pts (FY25) → implied ~57% (Q1 2026, no QoQ change reported)
shifts.note: Margins holding at elevated post-recovery levels; volume-led growth without price concessions

MXL.verdict: Infrastructure optical DSP cycle is inflecting faster than expected — Q1 Infrastructure growth exceeding all of FY25 is the key signal; easing customer concentration further strengthens the thesis.

NOW.headline: Consistent 22% growth with RPO +25% YoY maintained; Now Assist AI agents broadly deployed but consumption-model revenue not yet visibly inflecting the top line.

shifts.trend: Revenue: +21% (FY25, $13.28B) → +22% (Q1 2026, $3.77B YoY); subscription revenue +21% → +22%
shifts.note: Best-in-class SaaS consistency; no re-acceleration yet from AI agents at scale

shifts.trend: RPO: $28.2B +27% YoY (FY25 year-end) → $27.7B +25% YoY (Q1 2026 end); cRPO +25% → +23%
shifts.note: RPO declined marginally QoQ (recognition vs new bookings pace) but YoY growth stable; cRPO growth stable

shifts.trend: Self-hosted upfront revenue: $83M FY25 uplift → $157M (Q1 FY25) → $138M (Q1 FY26); Now Assist embedded across 10+ product lines both periods
shifts.note: Federal self-hosted demand moderating slightly; core subscription growing consistently

shifts.trend: Operating margin guidance 'expanding through restructuring and AI investments'; cash flow strong (implied from RPO/revenue mix)
shifts.note: Margin profile stable; AI agent revenue not yet material enough to move the needle

NOW.verdict: High-quality, consistent compounder — thesis is intact but needs Now Assist consumption revenue to inflect to re-rate; current 22% growth alone does not justify outsized multiple expansion.

NVT.headline: Systems Protection re-accelerated to +76% YoY with segment margin expanding to 22.7%; portfolio simplified to enclosures + liquid cooling + Electrical Connections after Thermal Management divestiture.

shifts.trend: Total revenue: +30% (FY25 organic +13%) → +53.5% (Q1 FY26 organic +34.4%); Systems Protection: +42% (FY25) → +76% (Q1 FY26)
shifts.note: Organic acceleration from 13% to 34% is the key signal — not just M&A contribution from Avail EPG

shifts.trend: Systems Protection segment margin: 20.7% (FY25) → 22.7% (Q1 FY26, +220bps)
shifts.note: Volume leverage on organic growth driving the margin expansion; data center infrastructure vertical +46pts of organic SP growth

shifts.trend: Portfolio: Avail EPG (electrical products group) acquired May 2025 for $1.0B → divested Thermal Management for $1.6B Jan 2025; net: adds enclosures/liquid cooling, removes thermal management
shifts.note: Portfolio simplification toward AI datacenter infrastructure is accretive to growth and margin profile

shifts.trend: Backlog: $2.3B at FY25 year-end (+$400M from $1.9B); substantially all expected to ship in 2026; orders exceeding fulfillment capacity
shifts.note: Supply-constrained demand signal from backlog build; data center infrastructure vertical is the driver

NVT.verdict: Pure-play data center enclosure + liquid cooling thesis delivering — 34% organic growth in Q1 FY26 is the strongest evidence yet; backlog build confirms sustainability through 2026.

OKLO.headline: First hyperscaler prepayment agreement signed (Meta 1.2 GW, Jan 5 2026) and DOE safety design agreement approved Feb 2026; opex burn accelerating from $49M (9M FY25) to $139M (FY25 full year).

shifts.trend: Order book: ~18,100 MWe (Q3 FY25) → Meta 1.2 GW signed prepayment (FY25 10-K); 12 GW Switch master agreement + LOIs with Equinix, Diamondback, Prometheus active
shifts.note: First binding hyperscaler prepayment is a milestone — moves from LOIs to actual advance payment, de-risking demand signal

shifts.trend: NRC Phase I readiness: 'no significant gaps' (Q3 FY25) → DOE Aurora Nuclear Safety Design Agreement approved Feb 2026; PDC topical report accepted
shifts.note: Regulatory milestones tracking ahead of schedule; accelerated NRC review timeline confirmed by PDC acceptance

shifts.trend: Operating cash burn: $48.7M (9M FY25) → $139.3M (FY25 full year); FY26 guided $80-100M opex + $350-450M investing
shifts.note: Burn rate accelerating as engineering, regulatory, and construction activity ramp; well-funded with $1.5B raised in FY25

shifts.trend: FY26 investing cash guided $350-450M; ATM $300M raised Dec-Jan; Tennessee Fuel Center roadmap $1.68B; Fluor (through LEU JV) for Piketon adjacent to Oklo campus
shifts.note: Capital commitments ramping rapidly toward first Aurora deployment; 2028 target maintained

OKLO.verdict: Meta prepayment agreement is the thesis-confirming event — but 2028 first deployment and $350-450M FY26 capex mean this remains a 2-4 year pre-revenue story; regulatory execution is the gating factor.

ORCL.headline: OCI cloud infrastructure re-accelerated to +84% in Q3 (from +68% in Q2) while RPO surged to $552.6B; 9-month capex hit $39.2B (3.2x prior year) funded by $42.7B in debt issuance.

shifts.trend: Cloud infrastructure (OCI): +68% (Q2 FY26, $4.08B) → +84% (Q3 FY26, $4.89B) YoY
shifts.note: Re-acceleration confirms the AI workload demand tailwind; constant-currency growth +81% in Q3

shifts.trend: Capex: H1 $20.5B (~3x prior year) → Q3 $18.7B incremental → 9M $39.2B (3.2x prior year $12.1B)
shifts.note: Extraordinary capex expansion; ORCL is one of the most aggressive spenders in absolute terms relative to its size

shifts.trend: RPO: $523.3B (Q2 end) → $552.6B (Q3 end); new contracts continuing to outpace recognition despite 84% OCI growth
shifts.note: RPO growth outpacing revenue recognition even at 84% OCI growth — demand pipeline extending faster than capacity

shifts.trend: Senior notes: $17.9B (Q2 FY26) → $42.7B+ through Q3 FY26 + $5B mandatory convertible preferred + ATM equity program launched
shifts.note: Aggressive debt financing of the capex build; leverage increasing materially; credit ratings and interest coverage are key risk factors

ORCL.verdict: OCI +84% acceleration with growing RPO confirms ORCL is winning AI cloud share — the leverage risk from $42.7B+ in debt issuance is the primary risk to the thesis but demand visibility is strong.

PLTR.headline: Revenue re-accelerated to +85% YoY in Q1 as operating margin expanded to 46% (from ~31%); commercial growth outpaced government at +95% vs +76%.

shifts.note: Significant re-acceleration — AIP bootcamp-driven enterprise expansion is scaling faster than the government base

shifts.trend: Operating margin: ~31% (FY25 implied: $1.41B / $4.5B) → 46% (Q1 2026, $754M / $1.63B); gross margin 80% (Q1 FY25) → 87% (Q1 2026)
shifts.note: Operating margin expansion of ~1,500bps in one year is exceptional; SBC-adjusted margin even higher

shifts.trend: Commercial as % of revenue: 46% (FY25) → 47% (Q1 2026); commercial growth +95% vs government +76% — commercial gaining share
shifts.note: Commercial catching government for the first time; signals AIP/Foundry landing in enterprise at scale

shifts.trend: No customer >10% of revenue in Q1 2026 or Q1 2025; US 71% → 79% of revenue (concentrated geographically but diversified by customer)
shifts.note: Customer base diversified; US revenue concentration rising but reflects government + US commercial strength not single-customer risk

PLTR.verdict: PLTR is executing the commercial AI scale-up thesis with high-conviction metrics — 95% commercial growth and 46% operating margin are not marginal improvements; this is a significant thesis unlock.

PWR.headline: Backlog grew $4.5B in a single quarter to $48.47B and RPO surged to $26.24B; Q1 FY26 revenue grew +26.3% with operating margin expanding 50bps.

shifts.note: Acceleration in Q1 confirms that end-market demand is strengthening, not plateauing

shifts.trend: Backlog: $43.98B (FY25 year-end) → $48.47B (Q1 FY26, +$4.5B in one quarter, +10.2% QoQ); RPO $23.76B → $26.24B (+10.4% QoQ)
shifts.note: Adding $4.5B to backlog in a single quarter while growing 26% revenue confirms demand far exceeds supply capacity

shifts.trend: Operating margin: 3.8% (Q1 FY25) → 4.3% (Q1 FY26, +50bps); gross margin 13.4% → 14.1% (+70bps)
shifts.note: Margin expansion while growing 26% — operating leverage on the data-center-driven Electric segment

PWR.verdict: The $48.47B backlog and $4.5B single-quarter addition confirm PWR is supply-constrained on data-center-driven electrical infrastructure — one of the clearest bottleneck plays in the power layer.

SNOW.headline: FY26 revenue $4.7B (+31%) with RPO jumping to $9.8B and $1M+ customers growing +27% — AI data workload demand driving enterprise up-market expansion.

shifts.trend: Product revenue: +29% (Q3 FY26 quarterly) → +31% (FY26 full year, $4.7B); total customers 10,996 (FY25) → 13,328 (FY26, +21%)
shifts.note: Full-year acceleration from mid-year quarterly pace; AI/ML workload consumption driving the growth

shifts.trend: RPO: $7.9B (Q3 FY26 end) → $9.8B (FY26 year-end, +24% sequential); $1M+ customers 576 (FY25) → 733 (FY26, +27%)
shifts.note: Large customer cohort growing 27% faster than total customers — enterprise up-market is the growth driver

shifts.trend: Stock-based comp: ~41% of revenue (FY25) → ~34% of revenue (FY26); operating cash flow $1.2B (FY26)
shifts.note: SBC dilution declining as revenue scales; cash flow improving even as net losses persist

shifts.trend: NRR: 126% (FY25 year-end) → 125% (Q3 FY26) → 125% (FY26 year-end); Forbes G2000 customers ~43% of revenue both periods
shifts.note: NRR modestly declining (as guided) but still excellent at 125%; enterprise cohort holding consistent

SNOW.verdict: SNOW is executing the AI data platform thesis — +31% growth with $1M+ customers +27% is enterprise up-market expansion, not churn; the RPO jump to $9.8B confirms demand visibility for FY27.

SNPS.headline: Revenue nearly doubled to +66% YoY in Q1 on full Ansys contribution; Design IP remains -6% and China -22% ex-Ansys continues, but leverage is declining as $3.5B term loan was repaid.

shifts.trend: Revenue: +15% (FY25, $7.05B incl 4.5mo Ansys) → +66% (Q1 FY26, $2.41B, full Ansys quarter); Design Automation +96% (full Ansys) vs Design IP -6%
shifts.note: Reported growth is Ansys timing, not organic acceleration; organic growth likely ~15-20% as before

shifts.trend: China revenue ex-Ansys: -22% (FY25) → ongoing BIS trade controls in Q1 FY26; Design IP (China-exposed) -6%
shifts.note: China organic business has been structurally pressured since the May 2025 BIS letter (even though rescinded July 2)

shifts.trend: $13.5B total debt (FY25 year-end) → $3.5B term loan paid off Q1 FY26; Processor IP business sold to GlobalFoundries
shifts.note: Active deleveraging: paying down term loan + divesting Design IP assets to focus; buyback resumption approaching

shifts.trend: Design Automation: +96% Q1 (Ansys full quarter); Design IP: -6% Q1 → expected 'muted' growth FY26 per guidance
shifts.note: Ansys acquisition transformed the DA segment; Design IP remains a structural challenge requiring roadmap execution

SNPS.verdict: Ansys integration is proceeding on schedule and deleveraging is faster than expected — but the Design IP weakness and China pressure are real; net thesis intact if leverage comes down and DA/simulation business synergies materialize.

VRT.headline: Americas accelerated to +53% in Q1 with gross margin expanding 400bps to 37.7%; backlog doubled in FY25 and continued growing as FY26 capex ramps 2x.

shifts.note: Americas acceleration driven by hyperscale data center demand; hybrid air+liquid + HVDC architectures adopted

shifts.trend: Gross margin: 36.3% (FY25) → 37.7% (Q1 FY26, +140bps); Americas operating margin: 24.4% (FY24) → 26.8% (FY25) → 27.0% (Q1 FY26)
shifts.note: Three consecutive periods of Americas margin expansion on operating leverage; mix toward liquid cooling and HVDC helping

shifts.trend: Combined order backlog: $7.2B (Dec 2024) → $15.0B (Dec 2025, >2x in one year); continuing to grow into Q1 FY26
shifts.note: Backlog more than doubled in FY25 — orders placed 'in advance of ability to fulfill them'; supply-constrained demand

shifts.trend: Capex: $226.4M (FY25) → $425-525M guided FY26 (2x); Q1 FY26 already $114M
shifts.note: Doubling capex to meet backlog commitments; management expressing confidence in multi-year demand durability

VRT.verdict: Backlog doubling, Americas margins expanding, and capex doubling confirm VRT is the premier data center power + cooling beneficiary — thesis strongly intact with clear evidence of structural not cyclical demand.
